_article: XXXIX.2 — wolves expand territory. XXXI.5 / XXXIX.5 — the Memory Metric canonical example: Week 1 Old Chen owns a dog; Week 3 wolf attack — dog dies; Week 5 Old Chen says 'Used to have one'; months later a child tells the story. This momentum schema is the engine of that memory.
_description: Momentum schema for a BeastPopulation entity acting as a predator pack (wolves, spirit wolves, or equivalent). The pack has a territory size, prey density, aggression level, and expansion/contraction trajectory. When prey in the territory declines, the pack expands — pressing closer to settlements. When the pack takes livestock or a villager's dog, that event enters the memory ledger, ages, and is retold. This is the canonical Memory Metric driver.

_design_notes.why_this_matters: This is THE schema that makes the Memory Metric (Article XXXI.5) testable. Old Chen's dog is not scripted to die in week 3. The wolf pack's territory-expansion momentum, driven by prey decline, presses the pack toward the village. A livestock-taking event fires when the pack's territory overlaps village grazing land. The dog dies in that event — unscripted. Old Chen's grief, the village's fear, the child's later retelling: all emerge from the memory ledger + relationship graph + this momentum. The world remembers because the trajectory produced the event, not because a designer scripted the beat.
_design_notes.coupling: Feeds: settlement_graph (livestock loss events), memory_ledger (the dog-death memory is written here), rumor_momentum (the story spreads). Fed by: ecology (prey density), season (winter presses packs toward villages).
perceivable_without_notification_example: Howls at night closer than last month — the pack is pressing in.
A villager's flock is smaller than it was — a taking happened, unreported.
Old Chen's dog is gone — he doesn't mention it unless asked, but the dog isn't at his gate.
Tracks in the snow at the village edge — the pack was here in the night.
A child retells the story of the dog months later, with details that have drifted (the memory has aged).

schema.pack_id: string — matches a BeastPopulation entity id

current_state.territory_polygon_id: string — the region entity the pack currently holds
current_state.prey_density: float 0.0–1.0 — wild prey in territory (deer, rabbits, etc.)
current_state.aggression_class: enum — timid | cautious | bold | raiding | rabid
current_state.livestock_pressure: float 0.0–1.0 — how often the pack takes village livestock (drives memory events)
current_state.distance_to_nearest_settlement_blocks: int — how close the pack's territory edge is to a settlement (closes as prey declines)
current_state.seasonal_modifier: enum — spring | summer | autumn | winter (winter presses packs toward settlements)

rate_of_change.territory_size_per_tick: float — positive when prey scarce (expansion), negative when pressured (contraction)
rate_of_change.prey_density_per_tick: float — declines as the pack hunts; recovers if the pack moves on
rate_of_change.aggression_per_tick: float — rises with hunger (low prey), rises in winter, falls after a successful take
rate_of_change.distance_to_settlement_per_tick: float — negative (closing) when expanding toward settlements
rate_of_change.headcount_per_tick: int — births - deaths (hunts, starvation, territorial disputes)

components.hunting_pressure: float — pack headcount * per-wolf consumption; reduces prey_density
components.expansion_drive: float — rises when prey_density < sustenance threshold
components.seasonal_pressure: float — winter raises expansion_drive and aggression

drivers: string — e.g. 'north_wolf_pack: 9 wolves, territory north_forest, territory_size regional, prey_density 0.28 (declining — the pack has over-hunted), aggression bold, livestock_pressure 0.34 (has taken two goats this season), distance_to_wang_family_village 240 blocks (was 600 last season — closing), season autumn trending winter'

thresholds.name: livestock_raiding
thresholds.consequence: pack begins taking livestock; each taking is a memory_ledger event (the villager who lost livestock remembers; the village hears; the story may spread as a rumor). This is the perceivable 'something is taking our animals' period.

thresholds.name: pet_or_villager_attack
thresholds.condition: prey_density < 0.2 AND aggression >= raiding AND distance < village_edge
thresholds.consequence: pack attacks a pet or, rarely, a villager. THIS is the canonical Memory Metric event: Old Chen's dog dies. The event is written to memory_ledger with witnesses, location, and the victim. The dog's owner's relationship_graph updates (grievance against the pack, fear). The story begins to age and will be retold.

thresholds.name: territory_collapse
thresholds.consequence: pack starves; survivors disperse (may become lone wolves or join other packs); territory becomes safe. The village's fear declines over time as the memory ages — but the story persists in the memory ledger and may be retold for seasons.

thresholds.name: territory_expansion_into_settlement
thresholds.consequence: pack effectively menaces the settlement; village enters a fear trajectory; patrols or hunters may be organized; the settlement_graph marks the village as threatened. This is the slow-motion siege that makes a village arm its farmers.

interruptible_by: Hunter or patrol culls the pack (lowers headcount; may end trajectory)
Player or NPC drives the pack off (raises distance_to_settlement; temporary)
Rival predator displaces the pack (territory transform)
Spirit event mutates the pack (spirit_wolf transformation; changes the threat tier entirely)
Village counter-raid (a canon-grade escalation; the village takes the fight to the den)
schema.perceivable_without_notification: string — REQUIRED (Art XXXIX.5). e.g. 'Howls east of the village, closer than last moon. Old Chen's gate has no dog at it this week — he does not mention it. A goat skeleton picked clean in the south pasture, less than a week old. A child in the village tells a newcomer, unprompted, about the dog that died — though she has the details slightly wrong, and the pack was three fewer than she says.' If empty, FAILS the Momentum Test.
schema.compiled_into: settlement_graph (threat edge); feeds memory_ledger (the canonical Memory Metric driver), rumor_momentum
schema.canon_ref: INFERRED (Art XV) — mortal villages and wolf packs are ambient Er Gen canon; the specific Memory Metric scenario (Old Chen's dog) is the Constitution's canonical test case (Article XXXI.5)
